svg.text: EyeMatics System Architecture — Four-Zone Model (Per Site)
DSF orchestrates inter-site data exchange | EMD is a local dashboard over locally available data
React SPA — queries only its own local backend
No audit write from client | No direct access to remote sites | Pre-filtered data only
Express server — reads only local repository
Auth Middleware (JWT validation) + Audit Middleware (auto-log every request server-side)
Local: HS256 | Keycloak: RS256 (JWKS) | Payload: { sub, role, centers } | Center enforcement at API layer
JSON Files: users.json (bcrypt) | quality-flags | searches | excluded | reviewed
public/settings.yaml — port, host, dataDir, auth (provider + JWT secret), dataSource, therapy thresholds
Audit: server-side writes only | auto-purge > 90 days | no manipulation endpoint exists
Inter-site orchestration layer — FHIR R4 + BPMN 2.0
1. Feasibility/cohort counts — distributed query, aggregate answers only
2. Data export — pseudonymized FHIR Bundle via encrypted transfer | 3. Distributed refresh — push updates to local repositories
Maps EMD requests → DSF Tasks | Tracks process state | Imports returned data | Surfaces workflow status
Data flow: Source Systems → ETL/Pseudonymization → Local FHIR Repository → DSF (for inter-site) + EMD Backend (for local dashboard)
OIDC / JWT Auth | Realm: eyematics | RS256
Phase 5: prepare middleware | Same JWT payload as local auth
Site-to-site trust | Process authorization | Transport security (mTLS) | Workflow traceability | Approval gates
User-facing authentication (local bcrypt + JWT or Keycloak) | Local audit trail (SQLite, immutable)
Center-based data restriction (server-enforced) | Visualization | Local business logic
EMD never talks to remote hospitals. Express reads only from local FHIR store.
DSF upstream populates local store. Clean separation: orchestration vs. presentation.
Each site sends pseudonymized FHIR payloads via DSF → local consolidated repository → EMD reads from that.
Future: Pattern B (federated query) for strict data-minimization scenarios.
Handler extraction, Express server, FHIR proxy, build scripts — EXECUTED
bcrypt login, JWT sessions, SQLite audit (immutable, server-writes only, 90-day retention)
users.json (bcrypt + role + centers), quality flags, searches, exclusions
Server filters data before client sees it — unauthorized center data never on the wire
JWKS validation, claim mapping, docs — same JWT payload, different signer
1. Login: POST /api/auth/login → bcrypt → JWT { role, centers } → sessionStorage
2. Data: JWT Bearer → authMiddleware (center check) → audit auto-log → handler → pre-filtered response
3. Audit: server middleware intercepts every API call → INSERT audit_log → no client write path
4. DSF: Source → ETL → Local FHIR Repo ← DSF BPE (cross-site sync) | EMD reads local repo only
Dashed outline = single site boundary — each university hospital runs this full stack independently
